_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies NJ as 'no document required' for ID. New Jersey has no-excuse vote-by-mail and mails every registered voter an absentee ballot application before each election. NJ's 2026 primary is typically the first Tuesday in June; TODO verify exact 2026 primary date. Registration deadline is 21 days before the election per NJ law. NJ has online voter registration via the MVC portal.

earlyVoting.notes: New Jersey requires 6 days of early in-person voting before each election. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
New Jersey requires 6 days of early in-person voting. Additional early voting hours may be required. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
New Jersey mandates at least 6 days of early in-person voting before each election. Locations vary by county. No-excuse vote-by-mail is also available. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: New Jersey does not prohibit phones inside polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot or other voters. Campaigning is prohibited within 100 feet of a polling place.
additionalRules: No-excuse vote-by-mail: all registered voters receive an absentee ballot application before each election; any voter may request and return a mail ballot.
Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. Unaffiliated voters may not participate in partisan primaries.
New Jersey automatically mails a vote-by-mail application to all registered voters.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request a mail-in ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse vote-by-mail)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 7 days before the election (received by county clerk); in-person applications accepted until 3 p.m. the day before
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county board of elections by 8 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days after
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive by poll closing on Election Day, or be postmarked by Election Day and received within 7 days after.
